rationale: Tried to break the test empirically via mutation probes on a /tmp extraction of 954d6ca8. (a) is NOT hollow: mutating expandSlots line 817 `model := r.model()` -> `model := r.Model` (validation still passes, only expansion regresses) goes red on the exact assertion `slots[0].Model = "", want "codex"` — so the family fallback is pinned at the expansion path itself, not via a side effect; removing model()'s fallback entirely also reds (a) at Load. (b) is NOT hollow: deleting the neither-set branch from validateReviewerEntries makes Load return nil and (b) fatals; tightening (b)'s assertion to the distinctive `sets neither "model" nor "family"` phrase still passes, proving the error it sees today is the genuine neither-set refusal, not a YAML parse or other branch. len(slots)==2 also blocks default-config leakage. One narrow, non-blocking aliasing: the count-branch error `panel.reviewers[0].count must be >= 1` would also satisfy (b)'s two substrings, but the fixture's count:1 makes that branch unreachable without a second independent bug. Comments match actual behavior; worktree clean.

findings.title: Mutation probe: subtest (a) detects expansion-path regression independently of validation
findings.detail: Changing expandSlots (config.go:817) from r.model() to r.Model — a mutation that leaves Load validation green — fails subtest (a) with `slots[0].Model = "", want "codex"`. The test pins the fallback where it matters, end-to-end through Load + PanelGateReviewerSlots("bead") -> resolveGateReviewers -> expandSlots.

findings.title: Mutation probe: subtest (b) detects removal of the neither-set refusal
findings.detail: Deleting the r.model()=="" branch from validateReviewerEntries (config.go:560-562) makes Load succeed and (b) fails on `expected Load to refuse {model: ""} with no family, got nil error`. A /tmp probe that additionally required the distinctive substring `sets neither "model" nor "family"` still passed, confirming (b) currently fires on the genuine neither-set error, not a parse error or other validation branch.

findings.title: Subtest (b) substring pair cannot distinguish neither-set from the count branch (theoretical aliasing, non-blocking)
findings.detail: The count error at config.go:564 (`panel.reviewers[0].count must be >= 1 ... recovery: ...`) contains both asserted substrings `panel.reviewers[0]` and `recovery:`. With the fixture's valid count:1 this branch is unreachable, so a spurious green requires two coincident bugs (refusal removed AND count validation corrupted). Optional hardening for a future pass: also assert `sets neither "model" nor "family"` in (b). Not required for this bead.

findings.title: Doc comments accurately describe the code
findings.detail: model() (config.go:149-154) does fall back to Family on Model=="", and validateReviewerEntries only refuses the neither-set case; the two new supersedes comments (config.go:140-148, 550-557) match this exactly. Commit touches only config.go (comments) + config_test.go; Reviewer.Model stays plain string; full ./internal/config suite green; bead worktree git status clean.
